description: Keyed tabular decoding - parsing keyed headers and entry rows into objects

tests.name: parses a keyless keyed header as a root object
tests.note: Root-form discovery (§5) routes the keyless keyed header to a root object, not a root array

tests.name: parses nested field groups in keyed headers
parses keyed headers with the pipe delimiter
parses keyed headers with the tab delimiter
tests.input:
servers[2:	]{host	port}:
  alpha: a	8080
  beta: b	9090

tests.name: parses a quoted entry key containing a colon
tests.note: The entry-row split is at the first unquoted colon; the quoted colon is key data

tests.name: treats a key-value line at the header's depth as a sibling after entries end
tests.note: A keyed scope ends only by dedent or end of input

tests.name: treats key-value-shaped lines at entry depth as entry rows
tests.note: Opposite polarity to §9.3: in a tabular array, a colon-before-delimiter line at row depth ends the rows; in a keyed scope it is an entry

tests.name: splits an entry row at its first unquoted colon before delimiter splitting
tests.note: The token before the first unquoted colon is the entry key, accepted as a literal key per §7.4 even though an encoder would quote it

tests.name: decodes the cell token [] as a string, not an empty array
tests.note: The §9.1 empty-array form does not apply inside entry rows

tests.name: accepts a declared entry count of zero
tests.note: Encoders never emit keyed headers for fewer than two entries; decoders accept any N with count checks

tests.name: parses quoted cells containing the active delimiter
parses a keyed header on a hyphen line
tests.input:
items[2]:
  - config[2:]{x}:
      a: 1
      b: 2
    status: ok
  - status: down

tests.name: ignores comment lines between entry rows
tests.note: Comment removal never ends a keyed scope, and a comment line never counts as an entry

tests.name: applies LWW for duplicate entry keys in non-strict mode
tests.note: Entry keys are sibling keys of the decoded object and resolve last-write-wins
